title: Charred Uruguayan Chivito Bamboo-Steamer Basket
description: A twist on the classic Uruguayan sandwich, featuring charred meat and crispy veggies, served in a bamboo steamer basket with a genmaicha tea infusion.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, mix together genmaicha tea grounds, kosher salt, and black pepper.
Brush the beef tenderloin with olive oil and season with the genmaicha tea mixture.
Skewer the beef, red onion, bell pepper, and zucchini, and brush with olive oil and season with the genmaicha tea mixture.
Thread the bamboo skewers through the beef and vegetables, making sure not to overcrowd the skewers.
Brush the cured ham with olive oil and season with the genmaicha tea mixture.
Wrap the bamboo skewers in foil and grill for 8-10 minutes, or until the beef reaches medium-rare.
While the beef is cooking, toast the caper berries in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, or until fragrant.
Once the beef is cooked, remove it from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the beef into thin strips and serve with the toasted caper berries, mixed greens, and cured ham.
Place the bamboo skewers in a bamboo steamer basket over a pot of boiling water and steam for 2-3 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Serve the charred beef and vegetables in the bamboo steamer basket, garnished with toasted caper berries and mixed greens.
Infuse the genmaicha tea into a cup of hot water and serve on the side for a soothing and aromatic accompaniment.
